FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Hooks

Classes

Component

Base for stateful components. Override Render(); use hooks in stable order each render.

ComponentEl

An element that embeds a child Component (with its own hooks/state) into a parent's render output. The reconciler instantiates the component once (via Factory), reuses it across renders of the same ComponentType, and reconciles its rendered output as this node's content. This is what lets the framework compose many stateful components rather than one root.

ContextProviderEl

Provides a context value to its subtree (the React Context.Provider). One child.

ContextStack

Reconcile-time shadow stack of provided context values. The reconciler pushes a provider's value before reconciling its subtree (incl. nested component renders) and pops after; UseContext reads the top. (Push/pop happen on the dirty reconcile path, never the zero-alloc paint half.)

Context<T>

A context channel with a default. Consumers read the nearest provider's value via UseContext.

Ctx

Fluent: Ctx.Provide(MyContext, value, child).

Embed

Fluent helper: Embed.Comp(() => new MyComponent()) embeds a stateful child component.

Flow

Fluent reactive control-flow: Flow.Show(() => open.Value, panel), Flow.For(() => xs.Value.Count, i => Row(xs.Value[i])).

ForEl

Reactive keyed list (the Solid <For>). Count / ItemAt read signals; the reconciler runs them inside a boundary effect and re-realizes the children through the existing keyed diff when the list changes — rows are created/moved/removed (and their state preserved by key), with no parent re-render.

FrameClock

A per-frame tick the host bumps (only while something subscribes, so it's free when idle). A tree-level concern that must poll each frame — e.g. an overlay driving a timed close animation to completion — reads UseContext(FrameClock.Tick) to re-render every frame for as long as it's mounted.

InputHooks

A host-owned registration surface a tree-level concern can hook into without the host depending on it. The host bridges KeyPreview into the input dispatcher's pre-focus key hook, and runs AfterAnimations after the animation engine ticks but before recording. Read the host instance via UseContext(InputHooks.Current).

MotionHooks

Reusable Fluent motion patterns built on the component's animation hooks. The entrance mirrors WinUI's implicit show transition (Offset From 0,24 + Opacity 0→1, 400/200ms, the real Fluent decelerate curve). Honors ReducedMotion.

ReactiveComponent

A signals-native component: Setup() runs ONCE at mount (the SolidJS model) — create signals, wire bindings (TransformBind/For/Show) and return the tree. The component never re-renders; fine-grained bindings update exactly the nodes that read a changed signal. Use this for the highest-performance UI; use plain Component when you prefer the re-render model (still granular).

Ref<T>

A stable, mutable per-component box that persists across renders (the React useRef container).

RenderContext

Per-component hook storage (ordered; rules-of-hooks = stable call order). In the signals-first model a component is a reactive computation (its render-effect); UseState<T>(T) is a Signal<T> whose only subscriber is that render-effect, so a setState re-renders ONLY this component's subtree (granular), and UseContext<T>(Context<T>) subscribes the render-effect to the provider's signal so a context change propagates to exactly the consumers that read it. High-frequency scalars get UseSignal<T>(T) bound straight to a node channel — no re-render at all.

ShowEl

Reactive conditional (the Solid <Show>). When reads signals; the reconciler runs it inside a boundary effect and mounts Then / Else as the condition flips — structural change only, no parent re-render.

Viewport

The ambient client size (DIP), pushed by the host each frame so components can adapt to available width (responsive layout / NavigationView display modes). Read with UseContext(Viewport.Size).

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
